title: Scams and Phishing
drDigitalIntro: Phishing is when criminals send fake emails or messages pretending to be a trusted company — your bank, Apple, or the government. They want you to click a link and enter your password or personal information. Warning signs: urgent language ('Your account will be closed!'), unexpected requests for passwords, sender addresses that are slightly off (appl-e.com instead of apple.com), and links that don't match the company's real website.

playgroundTask.instructions: One of these emails is a phishing scam. Click the fake one.

items.label: From: apple.com
items.preview: Your iCloud storage is almost full. You can upgrade your plan at icloud.com/upgrade. Monthly plans start at $0.99/month.

items.label: From: apple-security-alert.net
items.preview: URGENT: Your Apple ID has been suspended! Click here immediately to verify your account: verify-apple-id-now.net — or your account will be permanently deleted within 24 hours.

playgroundTask.fakeExplanation: Real Apple emails come from @apple.com — not 'apple-security-alert.net'. Scams use urgency ('permanently deleted in 24 hours') and suspicious links to panic you into clicking.

drDigitalSuccess: You spotted the phishing email! Look at the sender's domain and be suspicious of urgent threats. Real companies don't threaten you like that.
drDigitalHint: Check the sender's email address carefully. Does it end in the real company's domain?
